title: Missile-Maker Destinus Launches Short-Range Interceptor Program

title: Tech Disruptors: Lazard’s Bilicic on Power Demand in the AI Era
summary: “The searches that you’re doing on Claude require the consumption of energy and at some point the system should hit a breaking point,” says George Bilicic, Lazard vice chairman of investment banking and global head of power, energy and infrastructure. Bilicic joins Bloomberg Intelligence’s Alexandra Davidov and Rob Barnett on this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast to discuss why AI and data-center demand are reshaping the outlook for utilities, power markets and infrastructure investment. title: Alkegen Plans Prepackaged Bankruptcy in Deal for Fresh Cash
summary: Alkegen has signed a restructuring deal with lenders that will inject the specialty materials maker with new money and slash its debt load by about $3.1 billion.

title: Gilts Fall as Burnham’s Fiscal Flexibility Comments Spook Market
summary: Gilts extended their retreat as UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham said he will seek “any flexibility” in the government’s fiscal rules, stoking concerns his administration will add to the nation’s heavy debt load.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
summary: Oil swung sharply between gains and losses in a fraught session as traders grappled with a barrage of headlines that suggest a worsening of the Middle East conflict, as well as attempts to bring about a pause in the war.

title: Burnham to Seek ‘Flexibility’ Within Existing Fiscal Rules
summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham indicated he’d look at using what he called “flexibility” within the UK’s existing fiscal rules to fund his next budget.

title: INR/USD: HSBC Offers Indian Diaspora 19x Leverage on FCNR(B) Deposits
summary: When Moumita Shome landed in Dubai from Australia last month, the calls from bankers started almost immediately.

title: Netflix Returns to the High-Grade Bond Market After 2024 Debut
summary: Netflix Inc. is tapping the US high-grade bond market for the first time since its debut deal two years ago, as slowing sales growth fuels investor scrutiny of the company’s future.

title: Lionheart (CUB) Nears $400 Million Deal for Venezuela Oil Stake
summary: Lionheart Capital is close to a $400 million deal for a stake in a Venezuelan oil field, in what would be one of the first major private investments in the country’s oil industry since the US began easing sanctions.

title: BlackRock Eyes More Than $12 Billion Debt for Texas Data Center
summary: BlackRock Inc. is looking to sell more than $12 billion of bonds to help finance a data center campus in El Paso, Texas, according to people with knowledge of the transaction, the latest in a string of debt deals helping to fund tech companies’ massive investments in artificial intelligence.

title: Stocks Get Lift From Chipmakers as Oil Rally Wanes: Markets Wrap
summary: A rebound in chipmakers powered Wall Street gains, with stocks also rising as an oil rally faded on hopes for a revival of a peace deal between the US and Iran that has all-but collapsed amid an escalation of hostilities.

title: Goldman Sees Turkey Likely Tolerating Faster Lira Slide
summary: Goldman Sachs economists expect Turkey to allow a more rapid depreciation of the lira in order to prioritize external-balance stabilization over disinflation.

title: Clothing Brand Reformation, Backers Seek $239 Million in IPO
summary: Reformation Inc., a sustainable womenswear brand, and shareholders including its private equity backer Permira are seeking to raise as much as $239 million in its US initial public offering.

title: US Stocks Rebound From Selloff as Nvidia, Other Chipmakers Rise
summary: US stocks advanced on Monday, paring last week’s slump, as chipmakers rebounded amid a deluge of earnings reports that will test whether Big Tech’s profits can match high expectations.

title: Paris Corn Hits Seven-Week High After Ukraine Cargo Attack
summary: Corn prices reached the highest level in seven weeks in Paris as renewed tensions in the Black Sea stoked supply concerns.

title: Canada Financials Stocks Close In on Top Spot as Energy Momentum Fades
summary: Canada’s financial stocks are on the verge of overtaking energy as the best performing sector in the equity market this year.

title: Goldman Says Hedge Funds Sell US Tech Stocks at Record Pace
summary: Hedge funds pulled back from US tech stocks at a record pace over the past two months, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s Prime Services desk.

title: Gold Miner Cadillac, Backers Seek $258 Million in Canada IPO
summary: Cadillac Mines Corp. and some of its backers are seeking to raise as much as C$363 million ($258 million) in an initial public offering, adding to the mining-driven rebound of public listing activity in Canada.

title: Goldman Offers Preferred Stock Days After Post-Crisis Spread Low
summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is looking to sell new preferred shares just days after a metric determining risk and value in these securities hit its lowest level since the global financial crisis.

title: Shapoorji Closes One of India’s Biggest Private Credit Deals
summary: India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group on Monday completed one of the country’s largest private credit transactions, raising around 151 billion rupees ($1.6 billion), according to people familiar with the matter, in a sign that domestic investors remain willing to fund large leveraged deals despite volatile global markets.

title: Short Bets Against US Equities Hit Record as AI Risks Mount
summary: Bearish bets across US stocks are surging to a record in the face of market gains, reflecting anxiety about the staying power of a rally that has seen the S&P 500 jump 18% from late March.

title: Hormuz Crisis Flares Just as Fuel Markets Tighten
summary: With the US summer driving season well underway, gasoline pump prices have again surged past $4 a gallon.
